response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering fundamentals over 13+ years and has worked at scale with millions of requests per second, they lack critical domain expertise in the core areas driving this role. There is no evidence of experimentation platform design, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging systems, metrics/telemetry infrastructure, statistical analysis, or rollout risk management—all explicitly required for a Principal-level growth engineer. This would represent a significant career pivot into an unfamiliar domain.
strengths: 13+ years of progressive technical leadership with proven ability to design and ship systems handling millions of requests per second at scale
Demonstrated adaptability across diverse technology stacks and domains (web, mobile, desktop, GIS, AI tooling), showing strong fundamental engineering principles and problem-solving ability

Strong mentorship and thought leadership track record through founding multiple open-source projects used by organizations like Green Map, teaching workshops, and organizing engineering community activities
concerns: No demonstrated experience building experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature-flagging systems, or quality/bias detection infrastructure—these are core to the role
Missing evidence of data aptitude in metrics design, telemetry infrastructure, statistical analysis, or experimental methodology—explicitly required competencies
Resume does not show track record of designing foundational systems enabling multiple teams to work faster, nor cross-team stakeholder alignment and influence at scale typical of IC6 roles

key_factors.factor: Software Engineering Fundamentals & Production Scale Experience
